case_title: GREGORY T. WALKER MD VS. THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA
cause_of_action: WRITS OF MANDATE OR PROH., CERTI., ETC./ADMIN. AGEN

docket_entries.description: EVIDENTIARY OBJECTION AND MOTION TO STRIKE OR EXCLUDE EVIDENCE OR, ALTERNATIVELY, TO SANCTION PETITIONER'S ATTORNEY FOR VIOLATION OF RULE 3.1113(D), CRC FILED BY RESPONDENT THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA

DECLARATION OF DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL LYNNE DOMBROWSKI RE: RESPONDENT MEDICAL BOARD'S OPPOS TO MEMO OF P&A IN SUPPORT OF PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS FILED BY RESPONDENT THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA

LAW AND MOTION, 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER GREGORY T. WALKER'S MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS OFF CALENDAR IN LIGHT OF PETITIONER HAVING VIOLATED RULE 3.1113(D) OF THE CA RULES OF COURT BY FILING A 63 PAGE MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; WHICH GREATLY EXCEEDS THE 15 PAGE LIMIT IMPOSED BY THE RULE AND IS WITHOUT THE PERMISSION REQUIRED BY RULE 3.1113(E). PURSUANT TO THE RULE 3.1113(G) OF THE CA RULES OF COURT, A MEMORANDUM THAT EXCEED THE 15 PAGE LIMIT IS CONSIDERED IN THE SAME MANNER AS A LATE FILED PAPER. RULE 3.1300(D) OF THE CA RULES OF COURT PROVIDES THE COURT DISCRETION TO REFUSE TO CONSIDER LATE FILED PAPERS. PURSUANT TO THE FOREGOING RULES, PETITIONER'S PETITION FOR ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO PROVIDE PETITIONER OPPORTUNITY TO RE-NOTICE THIS MOTION IN CONFORMITY WITH THE CALIFORNIA RULES OF COURT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR # 8189 301/PJB
ORDER RETURNING ADMINISTRATIVE RECORD
REPLY MEMO OF P&A IN SUPPORT OF PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS [CCP 1094.5] FILED BY PETITIONER WALKER MD, GREGORY T.

MINI-MINUTES FOR NOV-17-2008 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301
EX PARTE APPLICATION FOR ORDER ALLOWING ADDITIONAL PAGES FOR MEMO IN SUPPORT OF PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS FILED BY PETITIONER WALKER MD, GREGORY T.

RE-NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS;, PROOF OF SERVICE, POINTS AND AUTHORITIES, DECLARATION FILED BY PETITIONER WALKER MD, GREGORY T. HEARING SET FOR JAN-30-2009 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, PETITIONER GREGORY WALKER'S RENOTICED MOTION FOR PEREMPTORY WRIT OF MANDAMUS IS DENIED. THERE IS A SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE IN THE RECORD TO SUPPORT RESPONDENT'S DECISION. HOWEVER, THE COURT WILL HOLD OFF SIGNING THE ORDER SUBJECT TO MR. ORMASA SUBMITTING TO THE COURT A CITATION OF A CALIFORNIA STATUTE WHICH REQUIRES THAT THE DISCIPLINE THAT HAS BEEN IMPOSED AND THAT ALL RIGHTS TO APPEAL HAVE RUN ON IS FINAL. MR. ORMASA WAS GIVEN 5 DAYS TO SUBMIT THE CITATION FROM THE TIME A PROPOSED ORDER HAS BEEN SERVED ON HIM. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN CSR# 8189

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PETITIONER WALKER MD, GREGORY T. TAKE NOTHING FROM RESPONDENT THE MEDICAL BOARD OF CALIFORNIA (JUDGMENT DENYING PETITION FOR ADMINISTRATIVE MANDAMUS) SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

ORMASA WAS GIVEN 5 DAYS TO SUBMIT THE CITATION FROM THE TIME A PROPOSED ORDER HAS BEEN SERVED ON HIM. PER DIEM FEES ASSESSED BY THE COURT. Prevailing party is to prepare a form of order.
